channel.title: Elisabetha Arnold Archives - Dauth Family Archive

image.title: Elisabetha Arnold Archives - Dauth Family Archive

item.title: c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 2
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 2 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 2
Letter from Elisabeth Dauth to Walter Cliffe regarding the death of his wife Elsie Dauth, sister of George Dauth and daughter of Elisabeth. Letter was found in George Dauth's possessions.

Transcription
"...notice to the other local relatives, since their addresses are not known to us. ; We will have to console ourselves with the thought that the soothing effect of time will work on us as well and ease our pain! ; With best regards from my family, yours, ; Elisabeth Dauth "

People
Elisabeth Arnold
George Dauth
Elise Dauth
Walter Cliffe

Details
Date: 1899-01-25
Location: Germany, Rhineland-Palatinate, Mainz
Comments: In 2021 I had the letter translated from Kurrent to English by two different translators.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [Germany, Rhineland-Palatinate, Mainz], 1899-01-25, p. 2
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/c-1899-elisabeth-arnold-letter-to-george-dauth-page-2/

The post c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 2 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 1
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 1 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 1
Letter from Elisabeth Dauth to Walter Cliffe regarding the death of his wife Elsie Dauth, sister of George Dauth and daughter of Elisabeth. Letter was found in George Dauth's possessions.

Transcription
"Mainz, January 25, 1889 ; Much esteemed Sir! ; The news of the passing of your wife, our beloved relative, has touched us painfully, and I'd like to express our deepest condolences to you on behalf of my family. ; We can relate to the pain and loss caused by inexorable Death, for it hasn't been long since we ourselves stood at a fresh grave. ; If you'd be so kind to give... "

People
Elisabeth Arnold
George Dauth
Elsie Dauth
Walter Cliffe

Details
Date: 1899-01-25
Location: Germany, Rhineland-Palatinate, Mainz
Comments: In 2021 I had the letter translated from Kurrent to English by two different translators.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [Germany, Rhineland-Palatinate, Mainz], 1899-01-25, p. 1
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/c-1899-elisabeth-arnold-letter-to-george-dauth-page-1/

The post c. 1899 – Elisabeth Arnold Letter To Walter Cliffe – Page 1 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1883-01-11 – Fort Collins Courier – Germany Floods
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1883-01-11 – Fort Collins Courier – Germany Floods appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1883-01-11 – Fort Collins Courier – Germany Floods
Article that provides residence information for Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth.

Transcription
"The floods in Germany are a source of great anxiety to many who came from near the scene of their depredations. Mr. Louis Dauth, the grocer was formerly a resident of Mayence, and his parents are now residing there. His uncle also has a lumber yard there, which is situated about half a block from the Rhine river, and probably now completely flooded, as at last accounts half the town was under water. After leaving Mayence, Mr. Dauth removed to Westhafen, near Worms, and about twenty-five miles north of Mannheim, where the floods are the worst."

People
Andreas Dauth (residence)
Elisabeth Arnold (residence)
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1883-01-11
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1883-01-11, p. 3
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1883-01-11-fort-collins-courier/

The post 1883-01-11 – Fort Collins Courier – Germany Floods appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 1914-1940 – Andreas Dauth Family Tree
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1914-1940 – Andreas Dauth Family Tree appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1914-1940 – Andreas Dauth Family Tree
Page 4 of George Dauths Family Record states that Andrew and Elizabeth Dauth had ten children by the names of Louis, Elsie (Elizabeth), Julia, Marguerite, Katie, Anna, Philip, Lina, Wilhemina, and George.

People
Andreas Dauth
Elisabeth Arnold
George Dauth
Louis Dauth
Elsie Dauth
Julia Dauth
Marguerite Dauth
Katie Dauth
Anna Dauth
Philip Dauth
Lina Dauth
Wilhemina Dauth

Details
Date: c. 1914-1940
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Comments: George Dauths Family Record is a genealogy book initially created c. 1910s by George Dauth, which contains the family trees of the Dauths, Yeatons, Albees, Jeremiassens, and Rasmussens. Based on handwriting samples it was compiled by several individuals most likely Elizabeth Dauth, daughter of George Dauth, and Anna Jeremiassen, wife of Leslie Dauth.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [USA, Colorado, Greeley], c. 1914-1940
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/andreas-dauth-family-tree/

The post c. 1914-1940 – Andreas Dauth Family Tree appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 1914-1940 – George Dauth Notes On Dauth Family Tree
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1914-1940 – George Dauth Notes On Dauth Family Tree appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1914-1940 – George Dauth Notes On Dauth Family Tree
Page 5 of George Dauths Family Record discussing family religion, details about Andrew Dauth, Louis Dauth, Katie Dauth, and George Dauth.

Transcription
"Luthern; Andrew Dauth - Postmaster of Westhofen, also teacher; Uncle Louis in '70 war with France. Male nurse. His leg shot. Father Andrew sent George to this country to escape military training in Oct 16. 1884. Katie also came at that time. George first went to Collins."

People
Andreas Dauth
George Dauth
Katie Dauth
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: c. 1914-1940
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Comments: George Dauths Family Record is a genealogy book initially created c. 1910s by George Dauth, which contains the family trees of the Dauths, Yeatons, Albees, Jeremiassens, and Rasmussens. Based on handwriting samples it was compiled by several individuals most likely Elizabeth Dauth, daughter of George Dauth, and Anna Jeremiassen, wife of Leslie Dauth.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [USA, Colorado, Greeley], c. 1914-1940
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/george-dauth-notes-on-dauth-family-tree/

The post c. 1914-1940 – George Dauth Notes On Dauth Family Tree appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 18
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 18 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 18

Transcription
"Westhofen is a suburb south of Dortmund on the Ruhr. History of Grandpa and Grandma Fitting taken from 3 sets of scraps of papers written by Aunt Eva who died July 8, 1979. Jacob Fitting and Lena Dauth went to school in Westhofen, Germany together when 10 or 12 years old. Mamma’s father was a teacher until she was 14. Papa helped his father who was a farmer. Learned the trade 4 years $75 (Farming or Locksmith) Went to Frankfort to work. Inducted in army age 21 – 1 year in [?]Butzbach[?] – 2 years in Darmstadt in gun shop. Then worked Hoffslosser Schmidt fixing locks in the castle. Mamma stayed home and learned cooking. Then worked with father who was postmaster in Westhofen. Went to Darmstadt to Princess Alice School. Got a job in millinery store in Darmstadt. [?] in Hessen 6 months. Married in Antwerp Belgium by mayor of city. Then remarried in Church 22nd and Lawrence in Denver. In 1880 came from Germany to Fort Collins for 2 weeks had 35 cents. Went to 23rd and Walton rented a $5 room. Got a job C & S from rider in car shop 10 hr a day 7 days a week for $9. Mamma washed dishes and made clothes. Lived at 23rd and Welton 6 months. Conrad Shaefer went to Gas Winter and asked about job. Papa got a job from Winter $2 a day 10 hours a day. (Agent [?]Shoemeeker[?]) Fence put up [?]."

People
Andreas Dauth
Elisabeth Arnold
Eva Dauth
Jacob Fitting
Karoline Dauth

Details
Date: c. 1949-1980
Location: USA, Colorado, Denver
Comments: From the Elizabeth Donohue Transcripts, a written family record by the daughter of Karoline Dauth. Document states Karoline Dauth learned how to cook at home and worked with Andreas Dauth at his postmaster job.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [USA, Colorado, Denver], c. 1949-1980, p. 18
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/elizabeth-fitting-family-history-page-18/

The post c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 18 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 1
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 1 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 1

Transcription
"History of Mamma and Papa and their business – the Pioneer Iron and Wire Works -Written by Lizzie Donohue. Jacob Fitting born March 24 – 1856 in Westhofen, Germany died Dec 9, 1948. Lina Fitting born June 23 – 1857 in a village near France (Sauerschwabenheim) died June 18 – 1947, mother’s maiden name was Dauth, her father was a Postmaster in Darmstadt, Germany. They were married in Antwerp Belgium came to Denver in 1880 went back to Germany in 1889 had a small store in Darmstadt came back in 1893, had seven children all here in Denver except Annie who was born in Darmstadt, Germany. My father’s partner was a Mr. Winter. The First iron fence was made by hand and was put up at the Denver Union Station a part of which is still standing. By the way it was paid for in all silver dollars. This article was in the [?] Journal. Pioneer Iron and Wire Works Co. closed after 69 years. Creators of some of the most beautiful ornamental iron to grace the roads of Denver and Victorian homes on Capitol Hill are Going out of business after 69 years of continuous service. Up for sale to liquidate the estate of Jakob Fitting who died last year at 93 in the Pioneer Iron and Wire Works located at 1433 market for the last 56 years."

People
Andreas Dauth
Elisabeth Arnold
Karoline Dauth
Elizabeth Fitting
Jacob Fitting
Anna Fitting

Details
Date: c. 1949-1980
Location: USA, Colorado, Denver
Comments: From the Elizabeth Donohue Transcripts, a written family record by the daughter of Karoline Dauth. Document states Andreas Dauth was a postmaster and that Elisabetha Arnold had Dauth as a maiden name.
Source: Dauth Family Archive [USA, Colorado, Denver], c. 1949-1980, p. 1
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/elizabeth-fitting-family-history-page-1/

The post c. 1949-1980 – Elizabeth Fitting Family History – Page 1 appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: c. 2020 – Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth Portrait
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post c. 2020 – Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth Portrait appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
c. 2020 – Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth Portrait
The Fort Collins History Connection online archive page for the "Andrew Dauth and Elisabetha Arnold Dauth" photograph.

People
Andreas Dauth
Elisabeth Arnold
George Dauth
Caroline Dauth
Louis Dauth (Ludwig Dauth)
Philip Dauth
Eva Dauth
Florence Dauth

Details
Date: c. 2020
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins, Fort Collins History Connection
Comments: Included description states "Andrew and Elizabeth Arnold Dauth. Parents of Lina Dauth Fitting, George, Ludwig, Phillip, Katie Sanger, Florence. 1 sister in Germany. Louis' parents never came to America. Daemstradt was hometown. (Postmaster and owned mercantile.)"
Source: Fort Collins History Connection [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins, Fort Collins History Connection], c. 2020
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/document/andreas-and-elisabetha-dauth-portrait/

The post c. 2020 – Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth Portrait appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: Portrait of Elisabetha Arnold
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post Portrait of Elisabetha Arnold appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
Portrait of Elisabetha Arnold
The only known portrait photo of Elisabetha Arnold.

People
Elisabeth Arnold (subject)

Details
Date: 1869-10
Location: Germany, Rhineland, Worms
Comments: Photo was found in a lockbox owned by George Dauth with the portrait of Andreas Dauth. Its similar composition with that of Andreas Dauth portrait dates it to October 1869 and photographed by Gebruder Guff Speier Strausse in Worms.
Source: Dauth Family Archive
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/photo/portrait-of-elisabetha-arnold/

The post Portrait of Elisabetha Arnold appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: Portrait of Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post Portrait of Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
Portrait of Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth
This is the only known photo of Andreas Dauth and Elisabetha Dauth (Arnold). Most likely taken in Westhofen or Mainz Germany.

People
Andreas Dauth (left)
Elisabeth Arnold (right)

Details
Date: c. 1890s
Location: Germany, Rhineland, Westhofen, home of Andreas Dauth
Comments: Copies of this photo were distributed amongst Andreas Dauth's children. One copy can be found in the Fort Collins Archive.
Source: Dauth Family Archive
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/photo/portrait-of-andreas-and-elisabetha-dauth/

The post Portrait of Andreas and Elisabetha Dauth appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
